name: System Design Latency Numbers
description: Jeff Dean's latency numbers (with a few modern updates) every engineer should know.

cards.example: Roughly 1 CPU cycle on a 2 GHz core.
Pipeline flush; 10× slower than a hit predictor.
About 14× an L1 hit.
Atomic compare-and-swap on the futex word.
200× slower than L1; the wall most CPU optimisations care about.
30× a memory reference.
Wire time only — kernel + driver add more.
NVMe is closer to 20–50 µs at the low end.
Bandwidth-bound at ~4 GB/s on commodity DRAM.

cards.term: Round trip within the same datacenter
cards.example: Two switch hops + NIC traversal.
1 GB/s sustained on a SATA SSD; NVMe is 3–5×.
Mechanical head movement; the original tax SSDs eliminated.
~50 MB/s sustained — orders of magnitude slower than SSD.
Bound by speed of light through fiber across the Atlantic.
Pacific cable hops add ~50 ms over US↔EU.
SYN → SYN/ACK → ACK before data flows.
Down from 2 RTT in TLS 1.2; 0-RTT is replay-risky.
Cold recursion through TLDs; cached lookups are sub-ms.
Within a single broker; replication factor 3 adds proportional latency.
Dominated by network RTT, not Redis itself.
Index lookup + page already in shared buffers.
First-byte latency; transfer adds bandwidth time.
